data.title: Microservices
data.description: YouTube employs a microservices architecture to enhance scalability and flexibility.
data.technical_spec: Each component, such as video encoding, recommendations, and user management, operates as an independent service.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: CDNs are critical for reducing latency and ensuring fast video streaming worldwide.
data.technical_spec: YouTube utilizes multiple CDNs to distribute its content closer to users.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Load balancers distribute incoming traffic across multiple servers to ensure availability.
data.technical_spec: Global load balancers are used to manage the distribution of user requests.

data.title: Data Storage
data.description: YouTube relies on distributed databases and object storage systems for data management.
data.technical_spec: Systems like Bigtable and Google Cloud Storage store vast amounts of user data and video files.

data.title: Frontend Frameworks
data.description: Frontend utilizes modern web technologies for dynamic and responsive UI.
data.technical_spec: YouTube leverages frameworks like Polymer and LitElement to deliver user interfaces.
